places.name: Fremont Troll
places.description: A giant Troll lurks under one of Seattle's oldest bridges.

places.name: Seattle Underground
places.description: A walk through the historic city center, under the streets of Seattle.

places.name: Ye Olde Curiosity Shop
places.description: A 100-year-old purveyor of curiosities, curios, and kitsch featuring mummies, shrunken heads, and a Fiji Mermaid.

places.name: Museum of Pop Culture
places.description: A museum dedicated to the history and exploration of both popular music and science fiction.

places.name: Seattle Meowtropolitan Cat Café
places.description: Make your reservation to have coffee with some kitties.

places.name: 'A Sound Garden'
places.description: A huge musical sculpture and other art installations on the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration grounds.

places.name: Kubota Garden
places.description: This semi-secret garden in south Seattle emulates a traditional Japanese garden using the plants of the Pacific Northwest.

places.name: Beacon Food Forest
places.description: This edible park is open to any and all foragers.

places.name: Blue Moon Tavern
places.description: This popular bar has been blurring the line between normal and edgy for more than 75 years.
